title: Erdős-Faudree-Rousseau-Schelp Problem (complete bipartite graphs)
statement_text: Determine, up to a constant factor, the size Ramsey number $\hat{r}(K_{s,t})$ for $s \leq t$.
context_text: Erdős, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp proved $\Omega(st^{2s}) \leq \hat{r}(K_{s,t}) \leq O(s^2 t^{2s})$ for $s \leq t$ and ended their foundational 1978 paper by asking for the asymptotic order of $\hat{r}(K_{s,t})$. Pikhurko later found an asymptotic formula for fixed $s$ as $t \to \infty$, implying $\hat{r}(K_{s,t}) = \Theta(s^2 t^{2s})$ for $t$ sufficiently large in terms of $s$, but with no quantitative bound on how large $t$ must be.
paper_contribution: Proves the improved lower bound $\hat{r}(K_{s,t}) = \Omega(s^{2-s/t} t^{2s})$ for all $s \leq t$ via a hypergeometric random coloring argument, and deduces $\hat{r}(K_{s,t}) = \Theta(s^2 t^{2s})$ whenever $t = \Omega(s \log s)$; the range $s \leq t = o(s \log s)$ remains open.
notes: PDF source — exponent in Theorem 1.1 extracted as 'S2-s/t' and may be slightly garbled; interpreted as $s^{2-s/t}$.
paper_title: Three early problems on size Ramsey numbers

title: Erdős-Faudree-Rousseau-Schelp Problem (book graphs)
statement_text: Determine, up to a constant factor, the size Ramsey number $\hat{r}(B_n^{(k)})$ of the book graph $B_n^{(k)}$ (the join of $K_k$ and an independent set of order $n$), as a function of both $k$ and $n$.
context_text: Erdős, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp proved $\Omega(k^2 n^2) \leq \hat{r}(B_n^{(k)}) \leq O(16^k n^2)$ for $n$ sufficiently large in terms of $k$, establishing quadratic dependence on $n$ but leaving a massive gap in the dependence on $k$.
paper_contribution: Fully resolves the problem by proving $\hat{r}(B_n^{(k)}) = \Theta(k \cdot 2^k n^2)$ for every fixed $k \geq 2$ and all sufficiently large $n$, closing the exponential gap in the $k$-dependence.
notes: PDF source — math notation appears clean for this result.
paper_title: Three early problems on size Ramsey numbers

title: Erdős-Faudree-Rousseau-Schelp Problem (starburst graphs)
statement_text: Determine, up to a constant factor, the size Ramsey number $\hat{r}(S_n^{(k)})$ of the starburst graph $S_n^{(k)}$ (obtained from $K_k$ by adding $n$ pendant edges to every vertex of $K_k$), as a function of both $k$ and $n$.
context_text: Erdős, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp proved $\Omega(k^3 n^2) \leq \hat{r}(S_n^{(k)}) \leq O(k^4 n^2)$ for $k$ fixed and $n$ sufficiently large, leaving only a $\Theta(k)$ gap between the bounds.
paper_contribution: Fully resolves the problem by proving $\hat{r}(S_n^{(k)}) = \Theta(k^3 n^2)$ for every fixed $k \geq 2$ and all sufficiently large $n$, confirming that the lower bound is tight up to a constant factor.
notes: PDF source — math notation appears clean for this result; starburst graphs appear to be unnamed in prior literature according to the authors.
paper_title: Three early problems on size Ramsey numbers
